page: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-16GB is a virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a profile suited for non-intensive workloads. The shared AMD EPYC processor delivers single-core and multi-core CPU performance in the bottom 10% according to stress-ng. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read performance at 33.08 GB/sec for large blocks, though write performance reaches average levels at 26.54 GB/sec. Computational benchmarks, such as Geekbench single-core, rank in the middle 50%, whereas multi-core execution, software compilation, and database operations fall into the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which limits sustained high-throughput compute tasks but offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-heavy, low-compute applications. It provides high memory density relative to its processing power, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring larger memory footprints without demanding continuous CPU performance. Suitable workloads include lightweight caching, small-scale memory-intensive applications, development environments, and basic text or image processing tasks.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. Utilizing a shared CPU allocation model, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks place its processing capabilities mostly in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU scores in the bottom 10% and memory read bandwidth in the bottom 25%. However, it maintains average performance in single-threaded compression and small-model text generation. This server represents a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for lightweight caching, development environments, and basic memory-intensive applications that do not require dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM. It is designed to provide high memory density for budget-friendly, low-compute cloud workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server offering high memory density with shared AMD EPYC processors for low-compute, memory-heavy workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
